macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Micron and Trip.com Earnings Set for June 24 as AI Demand and Travel Inflation Drive Focus
items.summary: Micron (MU) is scheduled to announce Q3 earnings with analysts expecting profits boosted by AI-driven demand for memory chips, while Trip.com (TCOM) reveals quarterly results amid 11% share decline and inflationary travel pressures.

items.title: U.S. New Home Sales Data for May Expected to Rise as Mortgage Rates Challenge Affordability
items.summary: Economists forecast an increase in U.S. new home sales at an annualized rate for May, reflecting how homebuyers are adjusting to higher mortgage rates and persistent affordability challenges.

items.title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer Resigns Amid Economic Crisis and Cost-of-Living Pressures
items.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation after two years in power, citing mounting public frustration over a stagnant economy and worsening cost-of-living crisis.

items.title: Nigerian Stock Market All-Share Index Surges to 250,000 as Economy Posts Consistent GDP Growth
items.summary: Nigeria's All-Share Index rose from 50,000 to over 250,000, creating wealth for 6 million investors, while the economy recorded positive GDP growth every quarter since May 2023, surpassing global averages.

items.title: SpaceX IPO at $2 Trillion Valuation Sparks Debate on AI Bubble 'Jumping the Shark'
items.summary: Analysts question whether SpaceX's $2 trillion IPO represents the definitive 'jump the shark' moment for the AI bubble, following a week of AI-driven stock market speculation.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Tests Critical $60,000 Support Amid Short-Term Pullback
items.summary: Bitcoin is trading near a major support level around $60,000, with liquidity building at $61.8K as the next short-term target; price recently confirmed a close near $62,500 before dipping.

items.title: Ethereum Approaches Oversold Territory as Price Hovers Between $1.5K–$1.6K
items.summary: Ethereum is nearing oversold conditions on the 3-day RSI, with strong support between $1.5K and $1.6K; the asset has declined approximately 32% year-to-date, outpacing Bitcoin's 11% drop.

items.title: XRP Risks Breaking Key $1.13 Support Level
items.summary: XRP is attempting to break below a critical support level at $1.13; if confirmed, bearish targets could follow, though the break has not yet been validated as of recording.

items.title: BitMine Immersion Technologies Likely to Join Russell 1000 Index This Friday
items.summary: BitMine, which holds approximately 5% of total Ethereum supply and is backed by Tom Lee, has qualified for inclusion in the Russell 1000 index; market speculation suggests it will be added after Friday's market close.

items.title: Trump Family Expands Crypto Fortune Amid 'MAGA Inc.' Report on Tech Profiteers
items.summary: A new CorpWatch report titled 'MAGA Inc.' reveals that the Trump family is expanding its fortune through cryptocurrency, while companies like Palantir and Amazon profit from mass deportation and data surveillance policies.

items.title: Viral Game 'CatchCat' Spawns Crypto Coin in 24 Hours
items.summary: A new game called 'CatchCat' has gone viral in under 24 hours, turning real street cats into gameplay elements, and has already spawned a dedicated cryptocurrency coin named after the game.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google Releases NotebookLM 2.0: Agentic AI Researcher Powered by Gemini 3.5
items.summary: Google launched NotebookLM 2.0, transforming it from a document-chat tool into a fully autonomous AI research agent that executes code, analyzes data, generates cinematic videos via Veo, and creates PowerPoint/Excel files using over 100 built-in software skills.

items.title: AI Becomes Critical National Asset Amid Power Bottleneck and $700B Hyperscaler Spending
items.summary: Curzio Research reports that AI is now a critical national asset, with demand outstripping supply due to a power bottleneck; top five hyperscalers are projected to spend nearly $700 billion on AI infrastructure, triple the 2025 estimate of $250 billion.

items.title: Global Markets Unwind AI Stocks as Investors Nervous Over Massive Sector Spending
items.summary: Global markets experienced a sharp unwind in AI and technology stocks, with major chipmakers facing heavy selling pressure as investors express concern over the sheer amount of capital chasing the AI sector, suggesting the trend needs to calm down short-term.

items.title: AWS CMO Julia White: 'The Only Way to Get Good at AI is to Fail at It'
items.summary: AWS Chief Marketing Officer Julia White emphasized that mastering AI requires iterative failure, reinforcing the industry trend that hands-on experimentation and learning from mistakes are essential for AI proficiency.

items.title: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman Acknowledges Original Developers of General AI Tools
items.summary: Sam Altman, CEO of OpenAI, publicly thanked the original developers of general AI tools, noting that general AI tools now outperform expensive domain-specific ones, highlighting a key industry trend in AI tool evolution.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: GTA 6 Pre-Orders Open June 25 with Free GTA 5 Upgrades
items.summary: Rockstar Games announces that pre-orders for Grand Theft Auto 6 will open on June 25, while also offering free upgrades to GTA 5 for players on older consoles and PC.

items.title: Netflix Launches New Smartphone-Controlled Horror Game for Immersion
items.summary: Netflix releases a new horror game that uses players' smartphones to control in-game stars, aiming for deeper immersion in the experience.

items.title: Epic Games Integrates Generative AI into Future Unreal Engine Versions
items.summary: Epic Games details its strategy to make generative AI a core feature in upcoming versions of Unreal Engine, enhancing development capabilities.

items.title: Xbox Studios Ninja Theory, Double Fine, and Compulsion Reportedly Closing
items.summary: Industry reports suggest that first-party Xbox studios Ninja Theory, Double Fine, and Compulsion Games are at risk of being shut down.

items.title: EA Creates New Division to Push In-Game Advertising
items.summary: Electronic Arts has established an entire division dedicated to increasing in-game advertising, marking a significant shift in monetization strategy.

items.title: Dave the Diver Announced for iOS and Android in August
items.summary: The underwater roguelite and restaurant management game Dave the Diver will launch on mobile platforms (iOS and Android) this August.

items.title: Creepshow Point-and-Click Adventure Game Arrives in August
items.summary: A new point-and-click adventure game based on the horror anthology series Creepshow is set to release this summer, specifically in August.

items.title: Roblox Globally Rolls Out Age-Gated Account Tiers for Child Safety
items.summary: Roblox has implemented new age-gated account tiers globally as a tactic to improve safety for children on its platform.

items.title: EU Rejects Mandatory Game Preservation Law
items.summary: The European Commission states it cannot force publishers to preserve video games due to existing copyright and intellectual property laws.

items.title: Overwatch Season 3 Hero Shion Throws Bikes at Players
items.summary: Overwatch's latest hero, Shion, arrives with Season 3 starting June 16, featuring a unique ability to throw bikes at opponents' heads.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Amazon Prime Day 2026 Begins with Major Deals on Routers, Tablets, and Laptops
items.summary: Amazon Prime Day has launched, featuring discounts of over 35% on Samsung's Galaxy Tab S10 FE+, 47% off OLED Microsoft Surface Pro laptops, and significant savings on TP-Link routers and mesh systems.

items.title: Apple Announces Incoming CEO John Ternus to Overhaul Device Design
items.summary: Apple has confirmed John Ternus, who previously led the company's design team, as its incoming CEO, with sources indicating he plans to push for 'truly incredible' new designs to break away from the current iPhone aesthetic.

items.title: US Green Energy Firms Face Potential Federal Funding Cuts
items.summary: Billions of dollars in US federal funding for green energy projects are at risk as the government prepares to cut support, causing US green energy firms to brace for significant financial impacts.

items.title: Hydration Tracking Gadgets Flood the Market as New Tech Trend
items.summary: A new wave of hydration tracking gadgets is entering the consumer market, marking a significant development in personal health technology and wearable devices.

items.title: Rockstar Games Responds to White House GTA-Themed Post
items.summary: Rockstar Games has issued a reaction to a White House social media post themed around the Grand Theft Auto video game series, highlighting a notable intersection between gaming culture and government communications.
